name_cn: High PV Generation with Low Load Scenario
name_en: High PV Generation with Low Load Scenario
description_cn: Simulates overvoltage, reverse power flow, and renewable curtailment caused by high distributed PV output and low system demand around midday.
description_en: Simulates high distributed PV generation under low demand.
typical_user_requests: Increase PV to 2x and reduce load to 70%
Train a model to absorb PV generation

global_ev_multiplier.description: Do not introduce EV charging demand unless explicitly requested.

validation_rules.message_cn: This is a high-PV scenario, but both global and time-varying PV multipliers are below 1.3, so the intended characteristics may not be visible.
The load multiplier is too high for a high-PV low-load scenario and may weaken the intended characteristics.
The PV multiplier is excessively high and may cause unrealistic voltage violations or infeasible power flow.
PV must remain enabled in a high-PV scenario; the current configuration conflicts with the scenario semantics.
A high-PV low-load scenario should normally cover the midday high-output period; the current time window may not match the scenario semantics.

validation_rules.condition: effective_pv_multiplier >= 2.5 and use_ess == false and use_sop == false
validation_rules.message_cn: Both ESS and SOP are disabled under high PV stress, leaving insufficient absorption or voltage-regulation resources and increasing operational risk.

result_interpretation_focus: A lower voltage compliance rate indicates overvoltage risk caused by high PV output.
Lower renewable curtailment indicates that the control strategy improves local renewable energy absorption.
A significant drop in EV charging satisfaction may indicate that the strategy over-prioritizes grid-side objectives.
